purpose: Hermetic resolver/NSS lab manifest. Declares the synthetic fake-root layout (hosts, services, passwd, group, resolv.conf, optional fake UDP DNS endpoint), the semantic kernels that parse and replay those files, the test scenarios that exercise them, and the fail-closed contract that no scenario may issue a real-network call. Each scenario carries strict/hardened mode coverage, an oracle, structured-log fields, host-glibc fixture anchors, and a fixture obligation pointing at the synthetic root entry that drives it. The execution runner materializes fake roots, runs only the declared semantic kernels, binds only loopback fake DNS endpoints, and emits current-commit JSONL evidence under target/conformance/nss_lab/.

freshness.source_commit_policy: Runner reports and JSONL rows must carry the current git commit; execution tests mutate this field to prove stale artifacts fail closed.

execution_policy.rationale: Default runs must be reproducible offline; opting in to real network requires the FRANKENLIBC_NSS_LAB_ALLOW_NETWORK env var so CI cannot accidentally exercise a remote resolver.

required_kernels.contract: Classify numeric IPv4/IPv6 input before files, nsswitch, resolv.conf, or DNS are touched.
Parse /etc/hosts aliases and return deterministic address rows when nsswitch selects files.
Parse /etc/services name/protocol rows and attach service ports to resolver expectations.
Parse passwd name/uid/gid/home/shell fields and compare files-only NSS expectations.
Parse group name/gid/member lists and compare files-only NSS expectations.
Parse nameserver, timeout, attempts, and search-domain controls for bounded fake-DNS replay.
Parse per-database source order and reject scenarios that require unsupported NSS backends.
Replay fake loopback DNS success, timeout, poisoning rejection, and search-list walks without real-network egress.

oracle_delta_policy.description: Each row records the semantic-kernel expected/actual comparison. Supported scenarios must emit oracle_delta.kind=none; unsupported lookup families must fail closed with nss_lab_unsupported_lookup_family.

files.rationale: Static host-IP map for hosts-only scenarios.
Static port/proto map; needed by getaddrinfo with a service name.
Static getpwnam/getpwuid backing for nsswitch=files passwd path.
Static getgrnam/getgrgid backing for nsswitch=files group path.
Single-line nameserver pointing at the fake UDP endpoint when DNS scenarios are active.
Pin the order of databases per scenario; default 'files dns' for hosts.

fake_dns_endpoint.rationale: When a scenario needs DNS responses (timeout, poisoning, NXDOMAIN), the lab driver binds an ephemeral UDP socket and writes its address into resolv.conf. Default 127.0.0.1:0 means the driver picks a free port; tests assert no traffic leaves localhost.

optional_skip_conditions.recorded_reason: Real-network probes are optional and disabled in hermetic CI; the default runner records loopback-only fake DNS evidence instead.

scenarios.description: getaddrinfo('127.0.0.1') and getaddrinfo('::1') must short-circuit numeric parsing with no nsswitch lookup, no resolv.conf read, and no UDP traffic.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: Numeric inputs must produce IN6/IN responses without touching DNS state; assert no UDP send was issued by the lab DNS endpoint.

scenarios.description: nsswitch=files only; getaddrinfo on 'hermetic.local' must resolve from etc/hosts to the synthetic IP.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: etc/hosts row 'hermetic.local 198.51.100.7' must round-trip through getaddrinfo without DNS fallback.

scenarios.description: nsswitch=files only; getaddrinfo on service 'hermetic-service' must resolve the synthetic etc/services tcp port without DNS or network fallback.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: etc/services row 'hermetic-service 4242/tcp' must attach tcp port 4242 to getaddrinfo service-name expectations.

scenarios.description: fake DNS endpoint answers A/AAAA for 'cached.example'; second query must hit the resolver cache and not re-issue UDP.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: First lookup issues 1 UDP send; second lookup within TTL issues 0; assert cache hit on second.

scenarios.description: fake DNS endpoint silently drops query; resolver must time out per resolv.conf 'timeout' option, not block forever.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: Set resolv.conf timeout=1 attempts=1; assert getaddrinfo returns within 2s wall clock with EAI_AGAIN/EAI_NONAME.

scenarios.description: fake DNS endpoint replies with mismatched txid / wrong-section answer; resolver must reject.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: Inject a response with txid=0xDEAD when the query used 0xBEEF; assert resolver does NOT cache or return the spoofed address.

scenarios.description: resolv.conf 'search a.example b.example'; query for 'foo' must walk the search list and stop at first NOERROR/NXDOMAIN response per glibc semantics.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: Fake DNS NXDOMAINs 'foo.a.example', responds for 'foo.b.example'; assert exactly two queries issued in the right order.

scenarios.description: nsswitch=files only; getpwnam('hermetic_user') must return the synthetic etc/passwd row; getpwuid round-trips.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: etc/passwd row 'hermetic_user:x:60001:60001::/home/hermetic:/bin/sh' must round-trip both name and uid lookups.

scenarios.description: nsswitch=files only; getgrnam/getgrgid round-trip the synthetic etc/group row.
scenarios.fixture_obligation: etc/group row 'hermetic:x:60001:hermetic_user' must round-trip both gid and name lookups.
